volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Self-Tormentor (Section 4.1.660 - Section 4.4.723)

sections.label: Section 4.1.660
sections.original: Nostra est , si ita est .
sections.translation: If it is so , she certainly must be ours .

sections.label: Section 4.1.660b
sections.original: Vivitne illa cui tu dederas ?
sections.translation: Is this woman living to whom you delivered the child ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.660c
sections.original: Nescio .
sections.translation: I don ’ t know .

sections.label: Section 4.1.661
sections.original: Quid renuntiavit olim ?
sections.translation: What account did she bring you at the time ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.661b
sections.original: Fecisse id quod iusseram .
sections.translation: That she had done as I had ordered her .

sections.label: Section 4.1.662
sections.original: Nomen mulieris cedo quod sit , ut quaeratur .
sections.translation: Tell me what is the woman ’ s name , that she may be inquired after .

sections.label: Section 4.1.662b
sections.original: Philtere .
sections.translation: Philtere .

sections.label: Section 4.1.663
sections.original: Ipsa est : mirum ni illa salva est , et ego perii .
sections.translation: ’ Tis the very same . It ’ s a wonder if she isn ’ t found , and I lost .

sections.label: Section 4.1.663b
sections.original: Sostrata ,
sections.translation: Sostrata , follow me this way in-doors .

sections.label: Section 4.1.664b
sections.original: Ut praeter spem evenit . Quam timui male
sections.translation: How much beyond my hopes has this matter turned out ! How dreadfully afraid I was ,

sections.label: Section 4.1.665
sections.original: Ne nunc animo ita esses duro ut olim in tollenda , Chreme !
sections.translation: Chremes , that you would now be of feelings as unrelenting as formerly you were on exposing the child .

sections.label: Section 4.1.666
sections.original: Non licet hominem esse saepe ita ut volt , si res non sinit .
sections.translation: Many a time a man can not be [*] such as he would be , if circumstances do not admit of it . Time has now so brought it about , that I should be glad of a daughter ; formerly I wished for nothing less .

sections.label: Section 4.2.668
sections.original: Nisi me animus fallit , haud permultum a me aberit infortunium :
sections.translation: Unless my fancy deceives me , [*] retribution [*] will not be very far off from me ; so much by this incident are my forces now utterly driven into straits ;

sections.label: Section 4.2.670
sections.original: Nisi aliquid video ne esse amicam hanc gnati resciscat senex .
sections.translation: unless I contrive by some means that the old man mayn ’ t come to know that this damsel is his son ’ s mistress . For as to entertaining any hopes about the money , or supposing I could cajole him , it ’ s useless ; I shall be sufficient triumphant , if I ’ m allowed to escape with my sides covered . [*] I ’ m vexed that such a tempting morsel has been so suddenly snatched away from my jaws . What am I to do ? Or what shall I devise ? I must begin upon my plan over again .

sections.label: Section 4.2.675
sections.original: Nihil tam difficile est quin quaerendo investigari possiet .
sections.translation: Nothing is so difficult , but that it may be found out by seeking . What now if I set about it after this fashion . ( He considers .) That ’ s of no use . What , if after this fashion ? I effect just about the same . But this I think will do . It can not . Yes ! excellent . Bravo ! I ’ ve found out the best of all — I ’ faith , I do believe that after all I shall lay hold of this same run-away money . [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.679
sections.original: Nulla mihi res posthac potest iam intervenire tanta
sections.translation: Nothing can possibly henceforth befall me of such consequence

sections.label: Section 4.3.680
sections.original: Quae mihi aegritudinem afferat : tanta haec laetitia oborta est .
sections.translation: as to cause me uneasiness ; so extreme is this joy that has surprised me . Now then I shall give myself up entirely to my father , to be more frugal than even he could wish .

sections.label: Section 4.3.682
sections.original: Nihil me fefellit : cognita est , quantum audio huius verba .
sections.translation: I wasn ’ t mistaken ; she has been discovered , so far as I understand from these words of his . ( Acvancing .) I am rejoiced that this matter has turned out for you so much to your wish .

sections.label: Section 4.3.684
sections.original: O mi Syre , audistine obsecro ?
sections.translation: O my dear Syrus , have you heard of it , pray ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.684b
sections.original: Quidni , qui usque una adfuerim ?
sections.translation: How shouldn ’ t I , when I was present all the while ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.685
sections.original: Cuiquam aeque audisti commodius quicquam evenisse ?
sections.translation: Did you ever hear of any thing falling out so fortunately for any one ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.685b
sections.original: Nulli .
sections.translation: Never .

sections.label: Section 4.3.686
sections.original: Atque ita me Di ament ut ego nunc non tam meapte causa
sections.translation: And , so may the Gods prosper me , I do not now rejoice so much on my own account as hers , whom I know to be deserving of any honor .

sections.label: Section 4.3.688
sections.original: Ita credo : sed nunc , Clinia , age , da te mihi vicissim :
sections.translation: I believe it : but now , Clinia , come , attend to me in my turn . For your friend ’ s business as well ,— it must be seen to — that it is placed in a state of security ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.690
sections.original: Ne quid de amica nunc senex .
sections.translation: lest the old gentleman should now come to know any thing about his mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.3.690b
sections.original: O Iupiter !
sections.translation: O Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 4.3.690c
sections.original: Quiesce .
sections.translation: Do be quiet .

sections.label: Section 4.3.691
sections.original: Antiphila mea nubet mihi .
sections.translation: My Antiphila will be mine .

sections.label: Section 4.3.691b
sections.original: Sicine mihi interloquere ?
sections.translation: Do you still interrupt me thus ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.692
sections.original: Quid faciam , Syre mi ? gaudeo : fer me .
sections.translation: What can I do ? My dear Syrus , I ’ m transported with joy ! Do bear with me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.692b
sections.original: Fero hercle vero .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I really do bear with you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.693
sections.original: Deorum vitam apti sumus .
sections.translation: We are blest with the life of the Gods .

sections.label: Section 4.3.693b
sections.original: Frustra operam , opinor , sumo .
sections.translation: I ’ m taking pains to no purpose , I doubt .

sections.label: Section 4.3.694
sections.original: Loquere ; audio .
sections.translation: Speak ; I hear you .

sections.label: Section 4.3.694b
sections.original: At iam hoc non ages .
sections.translation: But still you ’ ll not mind it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.694c
sections.original: Agam .
sections.translation: I will .

sections.label: Section 4.3.694d
sections.original: Videndum est , inquam ,
sections.translation: This must be seen to , I say ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.695
sections.original: Amici quoque res , Clinia , tui in tuto ut collocetur :
sections.translation: that your friend ’ s business as well is placed in a state of security . For if you now go away from us , and leave Bacchis here , our old man will immediately come to know that she is Clitipho ’ s mistress ; if you take her away with you , it will be concealed just as much as it has been hitherto concealed .

sections.label: Section 4.3.699
sections.original: At enim istoc nihil est magis , Syre , meis nuptiis adversum :
sections.translation: But still , Syrus , nothing can make more against my marriage than this ;

sections.label: Section 4.3.700
sections.original: Nam quo ore appellabo patrem ? Tenes quid dicam ?
sections.translation: for with what face am I to address my father about it ? You understand what I mean ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.700b
sections.original: Quidni ?
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.701
sections.original: Quid dicam ? quam causam afferam ?
sections.translation: What can I say ? What excuse can I make ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.701b
sections.original: Quin nolo mentiare .
sections.translation: Nay , I don ’ t want you to dissemble ; tell him the whole case just as it really is .

sections.label: Section 4.3.702b
sections.original: Quid ais ?
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.702c
sections.original: Iubeo ;
sections.translation: I bid you do this ; tell him that you are in love with her , and want her for a wife : that this Bacchis is Clitipho ’ s mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.3.704
sections.original: Bonam atque iustam rem oppido imperas , et factu facilem ;
sections.translation: You require a thing that is fair and reasonable , and easy to be done .

sections.label: Section 4.3.705
sections.original: Et scilicet iam me hoc voles patrem exorare ut celet
sections.translation: And I suppose , then , you would have me request my father to keep it a secret from your old man .

sections.label: Section 4.3.706b
sections.original: Immo , ut recta via rem narret ordine omnem .
sections.translation: On the contrary ; to tell him directly the matter just as it is .

sections.label: Section 4.3.706c
sections.original: Hem ,
sections.translation: What ? Are you quite in your senses or sober ? Why , you were for ruining him outright . For how could he be in a state of security ? Tell me that .

sections.label: Section 4.3.709
sections.original: Huic equidem consilio palmam do : hic me magnifice effero ,
sections.translation: For my part , I yield the palm to this device . Here I do pride myself exultingly , in having in myself such exquisite resources , and power of address so great , as to deceive them both by telling the truth : so that when your old man tells ours that she is his son ’ s mistress , he ’ ll still not believe him .

sections.label: Section 4.3.713
sections.original: At enim spem istoc pacto rursum nuptiarum omnem eripis :
sections.translation: But yet , by these means you again cut off all hopes of my marriage ; for as long as Chremes believes that she is my mistress , he ’ ll not give me his daughter .

sections.label: Section 4.3.715
sections.original: Tu fortasse quid me fiat parvi pendis dum illi consulas .
sections.translation: Perhaps you care little what becomes of me , so long as you provide for him .

sections.label: Section 4.3.716
sections.original: Quid , malum , me aetatem censes velle id assimularier ?
sections.translation: What the plague , do you suppose I want this pretense to be kept up for an age ? ’ Tis but for a single day , only till I have secured the money : you be quiet ; I ask no more .

sections.label: Section 4.3.718
sections.original: Tantum sat habes ? quid tum , quaeso , si hoc pater resciverit ?
sections.translation: Is that sufficient ? If his father should come to know of it , pray , what then ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.719
sections.original: Quid si redeo ad illos qui aiunt , Quid si nunc coelum ruat ?
sections.translation: What if I have recourse to those who say , What now if the sky were to fall ? [*]

sections.label: Section 4.3.720
sections.original: Metuo quid agam .
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid to go about it .

sections.label: Section 4.3.720b
sections.original: Metuis ? quasi non ea potestas sit tua ,
sections.translation: You , afraid ! As if it was not in your power to clear yourself at any time you like , and discover the whole matter .

sections.label: Section 4.3.722
sections.original: Age , age , traducatur Bacchis .
sections.translation: Well , well ; let Bacchis be brought over to our house .

sections.label: Section 4.3.722b
sections.original: Optime ipsa exit foras .
sections.translation: Capital ! she is coming out of doors .

sections.label: Section 4.4.723
sections.original: Satis pol proterve me Syri promissa huc induxerunt ;
sections.translation: To a very fine purpose , [*] upon my faith , have the promises of Syrus brought me hither , who agreed to lend me ten minae . If now he
